numerical_facts.context: Blue Bell ice cream has temporarily shut down one of its manufacturing plants over the discovery of listeria contamination in a serving of ice cream originating from that plant.
That includes 3-ounce servings of Blue Bell ice cream from this plant that went to institutions in containers marked with the letters O, P, Q, R, S or T behind the coding date.
The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.
It is the third time Blue Bell has taken action in light of a listeria outbreak at a Kansas hospital that served the company's ice cream.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
It later added 3-ounce cup servings to the recall.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
All five of them were hospitalized at the same hospital before developing listeriosis, the CDC said.
At least four of them had consumed milkshakes made with Blue Bell ice cream before developing the infection.
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
This has been the first product recall in the 108-year history of Blue Bell Creameries, the company said.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.
Cervical infections caused by listeriosis in pregnant women may result in stillbirth or spontaneous abortion during the second or third trimesters.
Cervical infections caused by listeriosis in pregnant women may result in stillbirth or spontaneous abortion during the second or third trimesters.

temporal_facts.context: Public health officials warned consumers Friday not to eat any Blue Bell-branded products made at the company's Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, plant.
The cup contaminated with the bacteria was produced at the Broken Arrow plant in April 2014, Blue Bell said.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.

In a separate outbreak in Texas, the CDC did find that listeria samples taken from patients who came down with listeriosis between 2010 and 2014 in a hospital that served 3-ounce Blue Bell cups matched the listeria in recovered samples.
In early March, in light of the Kansas listeria outbreak, Blue Bell recalled a group of products made at a plant in Texas.
Five people were infected and three died in the past year in Kansas from listeria that might be linked to Blue Bell Creameries products, according to the CDC.
In the United States, an estimated 1,600 people become seriously ill each year, and approximately 16% of these illnesses result in death.

explicit_negations.statement: The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream.
explicit_negations.context: The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.

explicit_negations.statement: Other 3-ounce servings were not made at the plant.
explicit_negations.context: The warning by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention does not affect other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings, not made at the plant.

explicit_negations.statement: The bacteria in the hospital and factory sample did not appear identical to listeria samples taken from patients infected in the Kansas outbreak.
explicit_negations.context: The bacteria in the hospital sample and the factory sample appeared to match each other genetically, the CDC said. But they did not appear identical to listeria samples taken from patients infected in the Kansas outbreak.

explicit_negations.statement: None of this means the ice cream is the source of either spate of the infections.
explicit_negations.context: None of this means the ice cream is the source of either spate of the infections.

explicit_negations.statement: This recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups.
explicit_negations.context: In a statement on its website, Blue Bell said "this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups."

absent_information.what: A definitive conclusion on whether the illnesses are related to exposure to Blue Bell products.
absent_information.why_notable: The article details listeria outbreaks and recalls linked to Blue Bell products, making the definitive causal link a crucial piece of information for understanding the full scope of the situation, which is explicitly stated as 'ongoing'.

corrections.wrong: One might assume all Blue Bell ice cream is affected by the warning.
corrections.right: The warning only affects products made at the Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, plant; other Blue Bell ice cream, including other 3-ounce servings not made at that plant, is not affected.

corrections.wrong: One might assume the listeria samples from the factory/hospital matched those from patients infected in the Kansas outbreak.
corrections.right: The bacteria in the hospital sample and the factory sample did not appear identical to listeria samples taken from patients infected in the Kansas outbreak.

corrections.wrong: One might assume the ice cream is definitively the source of either spate of the infections.
corrections.right: The document explicitly states, 'None of this means the ice cream is the source of either spate of the infections,' indicating the investigation is ongoing.

corrections.wrong: One might assume the recall includes all Blue Bell products, or at least common sizes like half gallons, pints, quarts, or other 3 oz. cups.
corrections.right: Blue Bell explicitly stated that 'this recall in no way includes Blue Bell ice cream half gallons, pints, quarts, 3 gallons or other 3 oz. cups.'
